vague_intent: Every weekday at 9am, collect yesterday's merged pull requests from our repo and post a standup summary to Slack.
true_intent: Exactly that: a weekday 9am standup that lists yesterday's merged PRs from our main repo and posts the summary to our #standup Slack channel. The only thing I didn't say is WHICH repo and WHICH Slack channel.
must_not_assume: a specific Slack channel without asking
notes: LOW-vagueness control. Trigger (weekday 9am), source type (merged PRs), and destination platform (Slack) are all specified — so the build must NOT re-ask those. Only the concrete repo + channel identifiers are missing. A correct build asks ~2 targeted questions, not a full interrogation. Tests calibration: ask only what's genuinely missing.
